description: The quality of contributing to collective wellbeing, harmony, and flourishing. Goodness is not an abstract moral virtue but the practical, tangible quality of actions and things that support the thriving of the whole.
rationale: Composed from the established roots 'we' (inclusive, collective) and 'loa' (path, way). The word's structure literally means 'our path' or 'the collective way,' tempering the concept of 'goodness' to mean that which is beneficial for the community. Its three-syllable structure and initial 'w-' sound make it phonetically distinct.
